document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MG, HAD1717MC
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC
standards: Council Directive 93/42EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation of safety and performance using literature review, equivalence, test reports, risk management, MAUDE, recall, and PMS information.
keyPoints: The device is a wired digital flat panel detector for general radiographic systems for human anatomy and is not to be used for mammography.
The report identifies the device classification as Class IIa and GMDN code 61108, Indirect flat panel x-ray detector.
The report claims equivalence to Radisen PEDRA_17FC and PEDRA_17FG devices based on intended use, technology, design, safety, EMC, and specifications.
The report states that no clinical investigation was conducted and that scientific literature was used for clinical evaluation.
The report concludes that the X-ray detector is safe and effective without added and unknown clinical evaluation risk.

evidence.evidenceText: File No A-CER-01 Rev. No 8 Date Sep/01/2025

evidence.claim: The device is a wired digital flat panel detector using TFT glass and scintillator materials CSI and GDX.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a wired digital flat panel detector... The detector is used in combination with a TFT glass and scintillator (CSI, GDX)

evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy... Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The report uses MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC, EN ISO 14971, and other harmonized standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The selected main methods and procedures are focused on MEDDEV.2.7.1 Rev.4 (June. 2016) Clinical Evaluation and Council Directive 93/42EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC... and EN ISO 14971 and other harmonized standards.

evidence.claim: The report states the device is undergoing initial CE marking.
evidence.evidenceText: whether the device is being developed/ undergoing initial CE-marking/ is CE-marked Initial CE-marking

evidence.claim: The manufacturer did not conduct a clinical investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: This product did not conduct clinical investigation.

evidence.claim: The report selected currently available scientific literature as the type of clinical evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Type selected Scientific literature currently available ●

evidence.claim: The equivalent devices identified are PEDRA_17FC and PEDRA_17FG manufactured by Radisen Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Identification of the equivalent devices and its manufacturer... 1 PEDRA_17FC Radisen Co., Ltd. 2 PEDRA_17FG Radisen Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the proposed products have equivalence for safety, clinical, and technical characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: Our product has equivalence about Safety and clinical, technical characteristics.

evidence.evidenceText: Electrical safety and performance certification test report... EN60601-1... EN60601-1-2... Performance report... EN IEC 62220-1 DQE test report

evidence.evidenceText: Sources of DATA A. http://www.sciencedirect.com B. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/ C. http://www.google.com

evidence.claim: The literature search date range was 2019.07.16 to 2020.03.03, covering literature from 2000 to 2020.03.03.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of search 2019.07.16 ~ 2020.03.03 Period covered by search: 2000 ~ 2020.03.03

evidence.claim: The report states that searched papers numbered 17, with 9 cited and 8 not cited.
evidence.evidenceText: 17 of the searched papers of which 9 were cited and 8 was not.

evidence.claim: MAUDE search results for product code MQB from Jan 01, 2023 to Nov 20, 2024 listed 20 malfunctions, 7 injuries, and 0 deaths.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Code: MQB... Report Date From: Jan 01, 2023 Report Date To: Nov 20, 2024... Device Malfunction 20 records... Injury 7 records... Death 0 records

evidence.claim: The FDA recall database search for product code MQB from Jan 01, 2023 to Nov/20/2024 found no records.
evidence.evidenceText: Events listed in the US FDA recall Database Product Code: MQB Date Classified from: Jan 01, 2023 Date Classified to: Nov/20/2024... No records
evidence.pageTarget: FDA Recall Database Product Code MQB

evidence.claim: The EU Medical Device Alert Database and Korea Medical Device Alert Database sections list no records.
evidence.evidenceText: Events listed in the EU Medical Device Alert Database... No records... Events listed in the KOREA Medical Device Alert Database... No records

evidence.claim: The report plans to update clinical evaluation using post-market data, with customer feedback, adverse events, side effects, and field failures collected every 3 months and CER revision considered yearly.
evidence.evidenceText: We collect information from delivered product such as customer feedback, adverse event, side effect and field failure with a 3months period and then consider revising CER with 1 year period.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the device is safe and effective and benefits outweigh risk.
evidence.evidenceText: The devices are safe and effective and the benefits of use outweigh the device risk.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is FDA, but the clinical evaluation basis is mainly MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 and MDD 93/42/EEC; FDA appears primarily in MAUDE, recall, and predicate certification references.
Potential conflicting sales evidence: the report lists sales quantities for Korea and non-EU overseas distribution in 2021-2024, but later states 'Complaints and Sales History - Not yet sold.'
MAUDE date evidence is internally inconsistent: the table uses Jan 01, 2023 to Nov 20, 2024, while the narrative says SEP, 2010 through MAR, 2020.
Some evaluator and declaration dates appear earlier than the Rev.8 document date, including Nov 20, 2024 and 2022/12/01.
OCR/text quality includes typographical and table extraction issues such as 'Safey', incomplete target user text, and inconsistent article counts.
